results.content:
Title: MIL-STD-810, Version: H, Date: 2019-Jan-31, Status: Active, Desc: ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING CONSIDERATIONS AND LABORATORY TESTS (31-JAN-2019) (65 MB)
MIL-STD-810H, DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE TEST METHOD STANDARD: ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING CONSIDERATIONS AND LABORATORY TESTS (31-JAN-2019) (65 MB)., This Standard contains materiel acquisition program planning and engineering direction for considering the influences that environmental stresses have on materiel throughout all phases of its service life.

results.title: MIL-STD-810 - Wikipedia
results.description: The standard contains military acquisition program planning and engineering direction to consider the influences that environmental stresses have on equipment throughout all phases of its service life. The document does not impose design or test specifications. Rather, it describes the environmental conditioning process that results in realistic material designs and test methods based on material system performance requirements. Finally, there are limitations inherent in laboratory testing that make it imperative to use proper engineering judgment to extrapolate laboratory results to results that may be obtained under actual service conditions.
results.content:
The standard contains military acquisition program planning and engineering direction to consider the influences that environmental stresses have on equipment throughout all phases of its service life. The document does not impose design or test specifications. Rather, it describes the environmental conditioning process that results in realistic material designs and test methods based on material system performance requirements. Finally, there are limitations inherent in laboratory testing that make it imperative to use proper engineering judgment to extrapolate laboratory results to results that may be obtained under actual service conditions.
In many cases, real-world environmental stresses (singularly or in combination) cannot be duplicated practically or reliably in test laboratories. Therefore, users should not assume that a system or component that passes laboratory tests of this standard also would pass field/fleet verification trials. Updated Test Methods and Procedures in MIL-STD-810H are listed below:
MIL-STD-810, U.S. Department of War Test Method Standard, Environmental Engineering Considerations and Laboratory Tests, is a United States Military Standard that specifies environmental tests to determine whether equipment is suitably designed to survive the conditions that it would experience throughout its service life.
Governs the simulation of vibration environments encountered by materiel during lifecycle phases, utilizing complex laboratory profiles such as random, sine-on-random, and helicopter scrambled vibration. In practical laboratory compliance, executing Method 514 profiles requires precise engineering calculation of dynamic force ratings (F = m ·
evaluate equipment's performance when exposed to a life cycle of environmental stresses · identify deficiencies, shortcomings, and defects in equipment design, materials, manufacturing processes, packaging techniques, and maintenance methods; and · demonstrate compliance with contractual requirements. MIL-STD-810G was replaced by MIL-STD-810H in 2019.

results.content:
NOT MEASUREMENT SENSITIVE MIL-STD-810H w/Change 1 18May 2022 SUPERSEDING MIL-STD-810H 31 January 2019 DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE TEST METHOD STANDARD ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING CONSIDERATIONS AND LABORATORY TESTS AMSC 10312 AREA ENVR DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A. Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.
This document provides guidance for developing standardized test methods to evaluate how military equipment withstands different environmental conditions, with an emphasis on tailoring tests to specific systems and their intended uses. It outlines management and technical roles in the environmental testing process, and describes test procedures in three parts - covering the tailoring process, specific laboratory test methods, and climatic data from around the world to consider.
It contains tailoring information, environmental stress data, and laboratory test methods. The environmental data contained in the Methods may help, but should not be used exclusively to define environmental stresses that materiel will encounter throughout its service life. This will help engineers to tailor analyses and tests to specific materiel and its defined life cycle. It is not valid to call out all of the Methods in this Standard in a blanket fashion for a MIL-STD-810H ENVIRONMENTAL DESIGN/TEST TAILORING GUIDANCE • ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING MANAGEMENT PLAN • LIFE CYCLE ENVIRONMENTA
DoD 5000-series documents call for component technology to be demonstrated in relevant environments to reduce risk on components and subsystems that have been demonstrated only in laboratory environments (DoDI 5000.02). results.title: MIL-STD-810 H Environmental Engineering Considerations and Laboratory Tests • MIL810.COM
results.description: Other types of testing include corrosion, contamination, altitude, ingress protection, and solar radiation. Some of the more specific tests are explosive atmosphere, acceleration, pyroshock, gunfire, ballistic shock and rail impact. Included at the bottom is the complete list of MIL-STD-810H test methods with links to descriptive pages.
results.content:
Other types of testing include corrosion, contamination, altitude, ingress protection, and solar radiation. Some of the more specific tests are explosive atmosphere, acceleration, pyroshock, gunfire, ballistic shock and rail impact. Included at the bottom is the complete list of MIL-STD-810H test methods with links to descriptive pages.
MIL-STD-810H testing is the test standard most commonly used to determine if a product can withstand the effects of difficult environmental conditions. Version H represents the eighth version of this military test standard. Even though MIL-810H is a military standard, many commercial products are tested for it.
The more common test methods include test method 516.8 shock and test method 514.8 vibration. These tests simulate the effects of jolts, drops and other similar actions that can occur during transit and operations. MIL-STD-810H test labs must have shock machines and vibration tables to complete this testing.
MIL STD 810H has 28 different test methods. Most products do not have to be tested on all 28 test methods. The type of product and end use of the product most often determines the test plan.

MIL-STD-810H testing is the test standard most commonly used to determine if a product can withstand the effects of difficult environmental conditions. Although MIL-810H is a military standard, many commercial products are tested to it. The term “ruggedized products” is often associated with devices that have met the requirements of MIL-810-H testing.
The more common test methods include test method 516.8 shock and test method 514.8 vibration. These tests simulate the effects of jolts, drops and other similar actions that can occur during transit and operations. MIL-STD-810H test labs must have shock machines and vibration tables to complete this testing.
Even though MIL-810H is a military standard, many commercial products are tested for it.

results.question: Which environmental factors does the MIL-STD-810 checklist address?
results.answer: The MIL-STD-810 checklist addresses a wide range of environmental factors, including temperature extremes, humidity, vibration, shock, altitude, solar radiation, salt fog, and sand and dust exposure.
results.title: Free MIL-STD-810 Environmental Engineering Considerations and ...

results.question: How often should the MIL-STD-810 checklist be used in the product development process?
results.answer: The MIL-STD-810 checklist should be used throughout the product development process, from initial design to final testing and validation, to ensure compliance at every stage.
results.title: Free MIL-STD-810 Environmental Engineering Considerations and ...

results.question: Can the MIL-STD-810 checklist be customized for specific products or applications?
results.answer: Yes, the MIL-STD-810 checklist can be tailored to specific products or applications, focusing on the most relevant environmental factors and test methods for the intended use of the equipment.
results.title: Free MIL-STD-810 Environmental Engineering Considerations and ...

results.question: What is the primary purpose of the MIL-STD-810 checklist?
results.answer: The primary purpose of the MIL-STD-810 checklist is to ensure that equipment and materials used in military applications can withstand various environmental conditions and stresses they may encounter during their lifecycle.
results.title: Free MIL-STD-810 Environmental Engineering Considerations and ...

results.question: Who is responsible for conducting MIL-STD-810 compliance checks?
results.answer: Environmental test engineers, quality assurance specialists, and project managers in the aerospace and defense industry are typically responsible for conducting MIL-STD-810 compliance checks.
